subject: Re: Export Sub-Tomograms : volumes
body:
Hi Arthur,

Great! It's best to use the nightly build then. Did the data have non-0 
phase shift by any chance? That would trigger a bug in 1.0.9.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Monday, November 30, 2020 at 9:45:55 PM UTC+1 artme...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Dimitri, 
>
> Thanks a lot for the reply.  I was not using the latest nightly build, but 
> the latest version of WARP 1.0.9. I went and installed the nightly build 
> and everything worked like a charm. Does that mean I should be using only 
> the nightly build? 
> To answer your questions: using WARP 1.0.9 I was able to reconstruct full 
> tomograms and export 2D particles through the subtomo dialog, which 
> resulted in a reconstruction in Relion that made sense.  
>
> Thanks a lot, 
> Arthur
>
>
>
> On Sunday, 29 November 2020 at 08:16:31 UTC-8 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Arthur,
>>
>> At this pixel size, the defocus-dependent mechanism I was suspecting 
>> doesn't make any difference, so I was wrong. Does reconstructing full 
>> tomograms at the same pixel size work? 
>>
>> You mentioned that exporting 2D particles worked – was that particle tilt 
>> series export through the sub-tomo dialog? If you export just one particle 
>> tilt series, and reconstruct it with relion_reconstruct, does the result 
>> make sense?
>>
>> 8 GB cards should be enough for 128 px boxes.
>>
>> You're using the latest nightly build, right?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Friday, November 27, 2020 at 8:10:12 PM UTC+1 artme...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Dimitri, 
>>>
>>> I tried with different subtomos sizes from 32 to 128 changing the output 
>>> pixelsize to 5.52 to 11.04. I also tried different particle numbers to crop 
>>> and it didn't seem to change. The original pixelsize of the tifs is 1.38 
>>> A/px and defocus is -2.5 um. Which and how many GPUs would you recommend to 
>>> not run out of memory in this case? 
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Arthur
>>>
>>>
>>> On Monday, 23 November 2020 at 13:44:32 UTC-8 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Arthur,
>>>>
>>>> what is the sub-tomo size, pixel size, and defocus? Does the error 
>>>> disappear if you reconstruct with larger pixels? Then it's probably the 
>>>> memory.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>> On Monday, November 23, 2020 at 10:06:09 PM UTC+1 artme...@gmail.com 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi Dimitry, 
>>>>>
>>>>> I'm having a similar problem when exporting subtomograms. I'm able to 
>>>>> export only particles, but not volumes. I'm running on a virtual machine 
>>>>> with 2 x 8 GB Tesla M60s GPUs. I tried running only on CPU but it also 
>>>>> crashed. Is there anything I'm missing? Do I need more GPU memory? 
>>>>>
>>>>> Here are the errors:
>>>>>
>>>>> “Lastcrash”
>>>>>
>>>>> 11/13/2020 18:33:39:
>>>>>
>>>>> System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed pipe.
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.
>>>>> CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.
>>>>> CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>>>>>
>>>>>  
>>>>>
>>>>> “worker_1_crash”
>>>>>
>>>>> System.Exception: cudaErrorMemoryAllocation
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\GPU.cs:line 
>>>>> 1175
>>>>>
>>>>>    at Warp.TiltSeries.ReconstructSubtomos(
>>>>> ProcessingOptionsTomoSubReconstruction options, float3[] positions, 
>>>>> float3[] angles) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 2130
>>>>>
>>>>>    at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 238
>>>>> On Wednesday, 19 August 2020 at 02:59:24 UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Fadwa,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I'm afraid CPU implementations of other steps won't be available 
>>>>>> anytime soon.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If you're running into memory issues with tilt series CTF estimation, 
>>>>>> you can lower the upper frequency used for the fit – memory consumption 
>>>>>> will reduce with its square. There is nothing like that to tune for full 
>>>>>> tomo reconstruction, unfortunately.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>> Dimitry
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Wednesday, August 19, 2020 at 11:28:20 AM UTC+2, 
>>>>>> fadwaf...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Dimitry, 
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thank you for the fast answer and explanation. 
>>>>>>> I will try the updated version, is the option to use CPU possible 
>>>>>>> for the other previous steps (tilt series pre-processing and full tomogram 
>>>>>>> reconstruction) ?
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thanks again,
>>>>>>> Fadwa
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Le mardi 18 août 2020 à 23:01:39 UTC+2, teg...@gmail.com a écrit :
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Hi Fadwa,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> it sounds like you might be running out of memory during 
>>>>>>>> reconstruction. I added a heuristic in 1.0.9 that automatically increases 
>>>>>>>> the reconstruction size depending on pixel size and defocus to avoid CTF 
>>>>>>>> aliasing. At high defocus and/or small pixel size, this can lead to very 
>>>>>>>> large boxes. You can try increasing the pixel size if you think the Nyquist 
>>>>>>>> limit won't be reached anyway. Alternatively, here is an updated version 
>>>>>>>> that lets you use the CPU for the most memory-intensive part of the 
>>>>>>>> reconstruction (check "use CPU" in the sub-tomo reconstruction dialog): 
>>>>>>>> http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/Warp_20200802.zip. Just copy 
>>>>>>>> and replace the files in your installation directory. Unfortunately, the 
>>>>>>>> CPU is much slower than the GPU at this task.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>>>> Dimitry
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Monday, August 17, 2020 at 11:56:11 AM UTC+2, FADWA FATMAOUI 
>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Hello Dimitry,
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> I am running into an error while trying to export subtomograms.
>>>>>>>>> Here are the steps I followed:
>>>>>>>>> - Generated STAR file from template matching in Warp.
>>>>>>>>> - Followed the user guide to export subtomorgams.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> But I am having this error: 
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> System.Exception: cudaErrorMemoryAllocation
>>>>>>>>>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions() in 
>>>>>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\GPU.cs:line 1175
>>>>>>>>>    at 
>>>>>>>>> Warp.TiltSeries.ReconstructSubtomos(ProcessingOptionsTomoSubReconstruction 
>>>>>>>>> options, float3[] positions, float3[] angles) in 
>>>>>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 2130
>>>>>>>>>    at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
>>>>>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 235
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> lastcrash.txt
>>>>>>>>> 8/14/2020 5:10:55 PM:
>>>>>>>>> System.Exception: Couldn't export the particles!
>>>>>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.TomoExportParticles(String path, 
>>>>>>>>> ProcessingOptionsTomoSubReconstruction options, float3[] coordinates, 
>>>>>>>>> float3[] angles) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\WorkerWrapper.cs:line 196
>>>>>>>>>    at 
>>>>>>>>> Warp.Controls.DialogTomoParticleExport.<>c__DisplayClass14_5.<ButtonWrite_OnClick>b__23(TiltSeries 
>>>>>>>>> series, Int32 gpuID) in 
>>>>>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\Warp\Controls\TaskDialogs\Tomo\DialogTomoParticleExport.xaml.cs:line 
>>>>>>>>> 457
>>>>>>>>>    at Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass43_1`1.<ForEachGPU>b__0() 
>>>>>>>>> in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:line 525
>>>>>>>>>    at 
>>>>>>>>> System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunInternal(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>>>>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>>>>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ThreadHelper.ThreadStart()
>>>>>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.TomoExportParticles(String path, 
>>>>>>>>> ProcessingOptionsTomoSubReconstruction options, float3[] coordinates, 
>>>>>>>>> float3[] angles) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\WorkerWrapper.cs:line 196
>>>>>>>>>    at 
>>>>>>>>> Warp.Controls.DialogTomoParticleExport.<>c__DisplayClass14_5.<ButtonWrite_OnClick>b__23(TiltSeries 
>>>>>>>>> series, Int32 gpuID) in 
>>>>>>>>> D:\Dev\warp2\Warp\Controls\TaskDialogs\Tomo\DialogTomoParticleExport.xaml.cs:line 
>>>>>>>>> 457
>>>>>>>>>    at Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass43_1`1.<ForEachGPU>b__0() 
>>>>>>>>> in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:line 525
>>>>>>>>>    at 
>>>>>>>>> System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunInternal(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>>>>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>>>>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>>>>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state)
>>>>>>>>>    at System.Threading.ThreadHelper.ThreadStart()
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> I tried to check the image series instead of volumes and it 
>>>>>>>>> generated the star file ( example: test.star) and the particle series 
>>>>>>>>> folder with the .mrcs 2D images.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Thank you very much !
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Fadwa Fatmaoui
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
